stories.title: EU countries eye setting up migrant ‘return hubs’ in Rwanda and Uzbekistan
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — A group of EU countries is considering sending rejected asylum seekers to Rwanda and Uzbekistan, three European diplomats confirmed, as the bloc moves to shift part of its migrant returns system beyond its borders for the first time. The plans follow the approval of a law  granting EU capitals the power to set up centers in non-EU countries for processing migrants who have been denied the right to stay in the bloc — so-called return hubs — provided those countries uphol

stories.title: Germany news: Pistorius set to scrap warship project — media
stories.summary: The defense minister is planning to drop a multi-million-euro project to build F126 frigates, media say. Train services have resumed after a communications glitch overnight. DW has more.

stories.title: Canadian police warn of possible copycat attacks after deadly shootout in Montreal
stories.summary: Assailant behind shooting that left three people dead wrote ‘incel’-like manifesto that was posted by a far-right outlet Police in Canada are warning of possible copycat attacks after three people died in a shootout in Montreal and the assailant’s lengthy manifesto, which called for “a new bloodletting”, was posted online by a far-right outlet. The document contains many of the hallmark grievances of the “involuntary celibacy” – or “incel” – movement in addition racist and misogynistic conspirac

stories.title: Poland, Ukraine: Dispute between two close allies deepens
stories.summary: Polish President Karol Nawrocki last week stripped Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy of Poland's highest state honor. How did it come to this? And what has it got to do with next year's election in Poland?

stories.title: „Olyan kövér a feladat, mint te” – ilyen tanári megjegyzésre is érkezett panasz az oktatási ombudsmanhoz
stories.summary: Összesen 1205 panasz érkezett 2025-ben, nagyrészt szülőktől.

stories.title: Szijjártó called Lavrov during Wagner mercenaries' mutiny to ask if he was all right
stories.summary: In the summer of 2023, the former Hungarian foreign minister anxiously inquired about the situation in Russia.

stories.title: Messiről kérdezték Ronaldót, legyintett, elfordult és egy másik kérdést kért
stories.summary: Húsz év alatt megkapta jó néhányszor ezt a kérdést, kicsit frusztrált lett tőle.

stories.title: Új szelek a magyar egészségügyben: beismerjük, hogy hibáztunk, de tanulunk belőle, hogy ne hibázzunk
stories.summary: Hegedűs Zsolt miniszter korábban egy itthon és egy Angliában meghalt gyermek esetén keresztül mutatta be a különbséget a hibáztató és a tanuló egészségügy között. Ha az egyik osztályon jobb az ellátás, mint a másikon, és ez nyilvános, a jó tapasztalatokat pedig megosztják, az a többi kórházban is motiválhatja a vezetőket és a dolgozókat a helyzet javítására.

stories.title: A Politico szerint a magyar kormány befékezett Ukrajna és Moldova csatlakozásánál
stories.summary: A lap úgy értesült diplomatáktól, hogy egyedül a magyar kormány nem támogatott egy lépést, amellyel az összes csatlakozási fejezetcsoportot megnyitnák.

stories.title: Kiemelten a parajdi bányával indokolták a romániai árvízkárok enyhítését az EP-ben
stories.summary: Az Európai Parlament szakbizottsága 14,3 millió euróról szavazott Romániának, a határozat kiemelte, hogy a tavalyi árvizeknél a legnagyobb károkat a parajdi sóbányában jegyezték fel.

stories.title: Nem mindegy, mikor fogyasztunk: új ritmus jön az energiában
stories.summary: Az energiaválság után a vállalkozások számára az áram már nemcsak egy költség, hanem stratégiai kérdés lett. Az árak ingadozása, az energiahatékonysági beruházások terjedése, a megújuló források térnyerése és az okosmérők megjelenése új szemléletet hozott a magyar KKV-k működésébe. De hogyan lehet a változó energiapiaci környezetből üzleti előnyt kovácsolni? Erről beszélgettünk Nagy Zsuzsával, az E.ON Energiamegoldások Kft. ügyvezető igazgatójával.

stories.title: Whistleblower investigating Ecuadorian president’s family business was murdered, activists say
stories.summary: Monika Silva Koniuszek died from a blow to the head and strangulation, a postmortem found, despite government claim of suicide Campaigners in Ecuador say a Polish anti-corruption activist who investigated allegations against the family business of the country’s rightwing president was murdered to silence her. Monika Silva Koniuszek, 41, was found dead in her home in Montañita, a coastal town in Ecuador’s Santa Elena province. The single mother of daughters aged four and nine, was found on the fl

stories.title: Cristiano Ronaldo megdöntötte Eusébio rekordját, és az első játékos lett, aki hat vb-n is betalált
stories.summary: A portugálok első vb-meccséhez nem sokat tett hozzá, Üzbegisztán ellen egy félidő alatt berámolt két gólt.

stories.title: Elmagyarázta Karácsony hivatala, miért nem jelent bevételkiesést, hogy betiltanák a közterületi reklámok nagy részét
stories.summary: Az óriásplakátokat betiltaná, a többi reklámfelületet nagyon erősen korlátozná a Főpolgármesteri Hivatal rendelettervezete.

stories.title: Az orosz földgázról már jövőre le tudunk válni, de így is finanszírozható a rezsicsökkentés
stories.summary: Napenergiában EU-elsők vagyunk, a legnagyobb kihívás viszont az épületek szigetelése. A Magyarország jövő időben új része az energiaellátás fejlesztéséről szól.

stories.title: Mindenki elfogadja, hogy a paradicsomnak és a kenyérnek nőtt az ára az elmúlt 13 évben, el kell fogadni, hogy az energia ára is folyamatosan nőtt
stories.summary: Hogyan használjunk kevesebb energiát, ha közben senki nem akarja feladni a kényelmét? Hogyan lehetne független energiarendszerünk? Fontos állítások a Magyarország Jövő Időben legújabb adásából.

stories.title: The EU's next seven-year budget could really bring the V4 together; according to Fico, they have common interests
stories.summary: The leaders of the V4 countries met in Gödöllő and afterwards they each confirmed their commitment to the cooperation and spoke about the important areas on which they intend to cooperate.

stories.summary: A volt magyar külügyminiszter 2023 nyarán aggódva érdeklődött arról, mi a helyzet Oroszországban.

stories.title: How 100 hospitals switched to pen and paper to defeat a national cyber-attack
stories.summary: For four days, dozens of Romanian hospitals went offline, as cyber-experts sought to defeat the hackers.

stories.title: Romania's Dan taps new PM, breaks democratic norms
stories.summary: President Nicusor Dan has nominated a new prime minister, without consulting Romania’s governing parties. Critics accuse Dan of breaking democratic rules.

stories.title: Zelenskyy returns Poland's highest honor as row deepens
stories.summary: Ukraine's president has returned a medal to Poland after a decision to strip him of the country's highest honor. Warsaw has been one of Kyiv's key allies since Russia began its war in Ukraine.

stories.title: Jenrick: ‘Burnham is Keir Starmer in a dodgy polo shirt’
stories.summary: LONDON — Reform’s Treasury spokesman Robert Jenrick describes the likely next Labour leader Andy Burnham as “continuity Keir Starmer” — but acknowledged that the newly elected MP for Makerfield will enjoy a honeymoon period if he ascends to No. 10 next month as expected. “He’s basically Keir Starmer in a dodgy polo shirt, and he stands for exactly the same kind of politics,” Jenrick said Tuesday in an interview for a special episode of POLITICO’s Westminster Insider podcast. The Reform

stories.title: Federal judge blocks Trump policy that allows immigration court arrests
stories.summary: Judge vacates administrations policies, finding actions of ICE and another government arm ‘arbitrary and capricious’ A federal ⁠judge in California vacated the ⁠Trump administration’s ​nationwide policies expanding arrests at immigration courthouses and the duration for detaining noncitizens in short-term facilities, finding the actions of US Immigration ⁠and Customs Enforcement and another government arm “arbitrary and capricious”. US district judge P Casey Pitts of the northern district of Cal

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Crimea locks down as Putin acknowledges ‘huge stream’ of Ukrainian drones
stories.summary: Rail bridge and power plant among latest infrastructure targeted by Kyiv in Crimea, where restrictions on public life have come to prevail. What we know on day 1,582 Continue reading...

stories.title: UK prioritised ties with UAE over averting mass atrocities in Sudan, MPs to be told
stories.summary: Foreign Office failed to act on warnings of genocide due to ‘pressure’ from emirates, Yale human rights investigator will tell a parliamentary select committee The British government had received intelligence that Ethiopia appeared to be supporting a genocidal militia in Sudan’s civil war as far back as 2024 but did not go public with the news for fear of upsetting the United Arab Emirates (UAE), a parliamentary committee will hear. In May 2024, officials from the Foreign, Commonwealth and Devel

stories.title: UK could rejoin EU on ‘short’ timeline if it wanted, former Brexit negotiator says – as it happened
stories.summary: Michel Barnier says UK could also join a new European security and defence council Whoever becomes the next UK prime minister will have plenty of political space to move closer to Europe, polling expert John Curtice has said. His comments come as many domestically and in Europe begin to question whether the potential future British prime minister will move further away or closer to the EU than Keir Starmer. “Labour’s vote is something like three-quarters to four-fifths pro-Rejoin [the EU] vote.

stories.title: Poll: UK voters wanted Starmer gone, but not an immediate Burnham coronation
stories.summary: Poll: UK voters wanted Starmer gone, but not an immediate Burnham coronation Results from The POLITICO Poll suggest that while U.K. voters were eager for new leadership, they were less interested in a shortcut for selecting the country’s new leader. By ERIN DOHERTY Illustration by Anna Wiederkehr/POLITICO (source images via Getty) Andy Burnham is the clear front-runner to become the country’s next leader after Prime Minister Keir Starmer  announced his resignation Monday  — a remarkabl

stories.title: Ukraine says major Crimea bridge destroyed in latest attack
stories.summary: Ukraine continues to hit infrastructure on the Crimea to isolate the Russian-annexed peninsula. Meanwhile, Russia attacked Volodymyr Zelenskyy's hometown with a ballistic missile in a deadly strike.

stories.title: Admiral fired in Hegseth purge wins Democratic primary in South Carolina
stories.summary: Nancy Lacore will spearhead effort to flip Republican House seat in November’s midterm elections A three-star navy rear-admiral fired by Pete Hegseth last year in the defense secretary’s purge of senior US military officials has won the Democratic primary in a closely watched congressional race. Nancy Lacore secured the party’s nomination for the US House of Representatives in South Carolina’s first congressional district on Tuesday after defeating Mac Deford, a US Coast Guard veteran, in a runo

stories.title: Progressive economists reject Unite leader’s criticism of Ed Miliband
stories.summary: Sharon Graham is urged to withdraw her claim that the energy secretary would cost jobs if he became chancellor More than 40 progressive economists have written to Sharon Graham, the leader of Unite, rejecting her claim that Ed Miliband would destroy jobs if he becomes the next chancellor. With Andy Burnham almost certain to be prime minister within weeks, there is a fierce debate within Labour about who should succeed Rachel Reeves at the Treasury, with Miliband considered a frontrunner. stories.title: Keir Starmer couldn’t beat the curse of Brexit – a politics poisoned by nationalism | Rafael Behr
stories.summary: The outgoing prime minister’s efforts to mobilise a healthier kind of patriotism fell flat. Andy Burnham may stand a better chance Britain is not ungovernable, but the chalice of high office has been spiked with unusually fast-acting poison. Six prime ministers down in a decade. The spectacle of the lectern planted outside No 10 for a resignation speech has acquired the familiarity of ritual. Since the Brexit referendum, the average tenure in Downing Street has been less than two years. That bal

stories.summary: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Ruston pushes for overhaul to controversial aged care tool Over to her portfolio, the shadow health and aged care minister, Anne Ruston , will introduce a private member’s bill to change the government’s controversial integrated assessment tool. The three things it seeks to do is to restore the discretion of a human assessor to make sure that the algorithm in their professional judgment doesn’t make an error. It

stories.title: Kenyan minister orders halt to construction of US Ebola facility
stories.summary: Decision comes after Aden Duale was held in contempt for ignoring previous high court ruling to stop work Kenya’s health minister told a court he had ordered preparations for a US-run Ebola quarantine facility to stop, after being held in contempt for ignoring a previous order to end work. Many Kenyans strongly oppose the facility , with deadly protests erupting since the complex was announced in May for US citizens evacuated from the Democratic Republic of Congo, which is grappling with a wides

stories.title: Turkey arrests more than 200 in crackdown before NATO summit
stories.summary: The Ankara prosecutor's office said 209 people were detained, while 32 others remained at large. Turkey has announced a ban on demonstrations amid preparations for next month's NATO summit in the capital.

stories.title: Chinese e-commerce giant Alibaba sues US government over defence blacklist
stories.summary: It is suing the US defence department after it was added to a list of firms with ties to the Chinese military.

stories.title: Eight sentenced to 450 years in prison over anti-ICE riot where officer was shot
stories.summary: An officer was shot in the neck during the Texas disorder by "Antifa Cell operatives", prosecutors said.

stories.title: Kenya orders immediate halt to controversial US-backed Ebola facility
stories.summary: The project had sparked court proceedings, public protests and questions about the country's role in responding to an outbreak beyond its borders.

stories.title: Irish government to contribute £197m to cross-border rail services
stories.summary: The money will come from the last allocation from the Irish government's Shared Island Fund.

stories.title: Andy Burnham lines up ex-Blair minister James Purnell as chief of staff
stories.summary: LONDON — Britain’s likely next prime minister Andy Burnham is lining up James Purnell, who previously served as a minister under Tony Blair, as his chief of staff in 10 Downing Street. The proposed appointment is at an advanced stage as Burnham plans for his expected entry to No. 10 on July 17, two people with knowledge of the plan told POLITICO. One of the two people, granted anonymity to discuss internal planning, said they believed Purnell’s appointment was “pretty much locked on.” The Times

stories.title: London climate week disrupted by … the climate
stories.summary: LONDON — Events at London Climate Action Week — one of the world’s biggest gatherings of green campaigners — have been disrupted by the heatwaves sweeping across the U.K. and much of Europe. Organizers said some of the approximately 1,000 events planned across the capital have been called off, amid “one of the hottest periods ever in the U.K.” “We are already seeing events being cancelled as much of London’s building stock cannot function,” Nick Mabey, CEO of climate campaign thin

stories.title: House gives final approval to bipartisan housing bill aimed at lowering costs
stories.summary: Donald Trump has indicated he will sign rare bipartisan initiative to tackle affordability after 358-32 vote in House The House gave final approval on Tuesday to a broad bipartisan bill aimed at lowering the cost of housing, with lawmakers in both parties eager to show progress on affordability issues ahead of this year’s midterm elections. The 358-32 vote sends the bill to Donald Trump, who is expected to sign it into law on Wednesday at the Capitol. The Senate passed the legislation 85-5 on Mo

stories.title: Chinese supercomputer leapfrogs best US machines to be ranked world’s fastest
stories.summary: China’s LineShine debuts at number one in Top500 – a list sometimes viewed as a national measure of global tech prowess A supercomputer in China now outranks its US counterparts as the world’s most powerful. It is the first time since 2017 that a Chinese computer has topped a list sometimes viewed as a measure of a nation’s technological prowess. The LineShine computer in Shenzhen displaced top-ranked US computer El Capitan in the Top500 rankings released on Tuesday. It was LineShine’s debut on

stories.title: Play puts spotlight on Kenya’s crisis of gender-based violence
stories.summary: Autobiographical work Free Me aims to encourage victims to speak out in country where violence against women is rising There are audible gasps in the auditorium in Nairobi as a husband launches a volley of blows and slaps on his wife and pushes her to the floor. “I wish I could spare you this,” the wife tells the audience. “My husband beat me up as if we were in a bar fight. Except, in a bar someone fights back.” The scene comes from Free Me, an autobiographical play by Gathoni Kimuyu, a Kenyan

stories.title: Mamdani-backed candidates sweep Democratic primaries in New York City
stories.summary: JFK’s grandson Jack Schlossberg fails to advance in election to replace Jerry Nadler in Manhattan district Zohran Mamdani ’s growing influence over the Democratic party was on show in New York City on Tuesday as three congressional candidates endorsed by New York’s democratic socialist mayor won closely watched primaries, while voters in Maryland, Utah and South Carolina cast ballots in primaries and runoffs. Brad Lander, the former New York City comptroller who also ran for mayor last year befo

stories.title: Documenting Ireland’s vanishing boglands: ‘They hold millennia in their layers’
stories.summary: Photographer Shane Hynan explores the tension between the central role peat bogs play in Irish life and their wider environmental impact “You can read Ireland’s history in the boglands . They hold millennia in their layers,” says photographer Shane Hynan of his project, Beofhód ( meaning Beneath in English). The boglands, known as portachs in Irish, cover roughly 1.2m to 1.5m hectares or about 14% to 17% of the country’s total land area. The raised bogs of the Irish Midlands are made of peat tha

stories.title: ‘The soul’s been ripped out of it’: Birmingham community housing scheme on brink over costs dispute
stories.summary: Row over ‘landmark’ development in Stirchley has left people homeless and put businesses at risk, say locals A housing development in Birmingham, touted as a model for community-owned housing in the UK, is at “serious risk of collapse” due to a dispute over construction costs. The Stirchley Cooperative Development (SCD), which was founded by local residents and businesses in Birmingham in 2016, was meant to provide 39 affordable and landlord-free homes owned and run by the people who live and wo

stories.title: Former WH Smith’s small suppliers to lose at least half of debts in rescue plan
stories.summary: If TG Jones’s aggressive restructuring is voted through, the charity Help for Heroes and other creditors will be out of pocket Small suppliers including the charity Help for Heroes are to lose at least half the money owed to them by the former WH Smith high street chain if a planned restructure is voted through this week. The books to paperclips retailer, which has 450 stores, was bought by the private equity firm Modella Capital last year and rebranded TG Jones. It has said it is likely that it

stories.title: Darren Jones says he will not challenge Andy Burnham for Labour leadership
stories.summary: Chief secretary to PM says he had been ‘reassured’ about Burnham’s economic plans after conversation with him Andy Burnham has moved a step closer to becoming prime minister after Darren Jones, the chief secretary to the prime minister, said he would not stand in a Labour leadership contest. Jones, who had been mooted as a candidate who could put Burnham’s ideas to a test in a race, told Sky News that he had had a “reassuring conversation” with the newly elected MP for Makerfield about his econo

stories.title: Philippines blocks GoreBox gaming app after school shooting kills three, injures 20
stories.summary: Investigators said one of the teenagers who allegedly opened fire on students at a high school had regularly used the GoreBox gaming app Philippine authorities have temporarily blocked the online gaming app GoreBox days after a rare school shooting in the south-east Asian nation killed three students and injured 20 others. Investigators said that of the two teenagers accused of opening fire on students at San Jose National high school in Tacloban city, one had regularly played the game, which al

stories.title: Dettol apologises after ‘toxic men’ advert sparks backlash in China
stories.summary: British disinfectant brand withdraws advert about a man’s efforts to find a ‘clean and untouched’ woman The British hygiene brand Dettol has apologised after an advertisement released in China, which it said was intended to criticise “toxic men”, was widely condemned on social media as offensive to women. The five-minute advert for a multipurpose disinfectant, released across many online platforms at the end of May, features a man comparing his girlfriend with his former partner. Learning that h

stories.title: Woman attacked by shark at Coogee beach wakes briefly from coma to say three words
stories.summary: ‘I love you,’ Leah Stewart told her mother and partner after coming out of a coma on Tuesday Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A woman attacked by a shark at Sydney’s Coogee beach has uttered her first words after waking from a coma 10 days since sustaining her injuries. “I love you,” Leah Stewart told her mother and partner after coming out of a coma on Tuesday. She was bitten by a great white shark on 13 June . C

stories.summary: State budget suggests Minns government is addicted to pokies revenue, with tax take predicted to jump from $1.6bn to $2.2bn annually within four years Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Anti-gambling advocates say surging tax revenue from poker machines in New South Wales is a betrayal of the Minns government’s promise to reform the industry. stories.title: Heatwave pushes Great Britain’s grid operator to call for extra electricity from power plants
stories.summary: Neso issues rare summer warning for Wednesday evening, as households turn on fans and air conditioning Great Britain’s grid operator has released a rare summer power supply warning for Wednesday evening as the heatwave is expected to get more intense , putting pressure on the energy system. stories.summary: An additional case is suspected in Western Australia, which would bring the total number of bird flu infections to four Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Deadly H5 bird flu has been confirmed in two Australian states after a migratory seabird tested positive for the disease in South Australia. stories.title: Former Liberal vice-president Teena McQueen dies from illness weeks after defecting to One Nation
stories.summary: McQueen was a long-serving member of the Liberal party before defecting to One Nation in May Former Liberal heavyweight Teena McQueen is being remembered for her contribution to the party despite her defection to One Nation. McQueen, who served as the federal Liberal Party vice president from 2017 to 2023, died on Wednesday after battling severe sickness. Continue reading...

stories.title: Europe’s media look on in bemusement at post-Brexit ‘revolving door’ of UK prime ministers
stories.summary: Front pages across the continent reflect on Britain’s political turmoil after Starmer becomes sixth prime minister to quit since 2016 In Germany, Downing Street was likened to a transit station, given the regular comings and goings of different prime ministers and staff. Meanwhile, a bemused Spanish newspaper concluded No 10 seemed to have been fitted with a revolving door. stories.title: Former Pinochet agents convicted over 1976 Washington DC carbomb murder
stories.summary: Attack targeted former Chile ambassador Orlando Letelier and his US colleague Ronni Karpen Moffitt Fifty years after Gen Augusto Pinochet ’s secret police detonated a car bomb in the heart of Washington DC , killing Orlando Letelier, a former Chilean minister and ambassador to the US, and his American colleague Ronni Karpen Moffitt, a Santiago court has convicted three former agents of Moffitt’s murder. stories.title: Middle East crisis: Rubio and Vance hold call with Lebanon’s president; Trump claims Iran has agreed to nuclear inspections ‘long into future’ – as it happened

stories.title: Israel continues to commit genocide by targeting children in Gaza, UN inquiry finds
stories.summary: Independent report says by aiming at children Israel is undermining capacity of Palestinian people to exist Israel continues to commit genocide by deliberately targeting Palestinian children in Gaza, an ⁠independent UN inquiry has found. The report by the UN independent international commission of inquiry examined violations against Palestinian children since the start of the war in Gaza, and said about 30% of the people killed by Israeli forces have been children. Continue reading...

stories.title: Trains resume after radio issue resolved, Deutsche Bahn says
stories.summary: Train services across Germany have resumed following a technical meltdown on Tuesday night. But regional and suburban rail operators warned that continued delays were possible into Wednesday morning.

stories.title: US: Google's YouTube settles teen mental health lawsuit
stories.summary: A Florida teenager said YouTube's addictive design contributed to depression, anxiety and sleep loss. Instagram, Snapchat and TikTok are also facing similar allegations about their impact on young users.

stories.title: US: Senate vote calls for an end to Trump's Iran war in rare bipartisan rebuke
stories.summary: Trump criticized the largely symbolic Senate resolution as "meaningless" after several Republicans joined Democrats in a rare break with the White House.

stories.title: How Africa's youth wants to save democracy
stories.summary: Africa's young population often finds itself facing aging, autocratic rulers. Unwilling to settle for democratic rituals alone, many are searching for creative new ways to shape their future.

stories.title: India: What’s driving the intense pressure on students?
stories.summary: The reported deaths of aspiring medical students in India have exposed the immense pressures of the country's high-stakes examination system and a worsening mental-health crisis.

stories.title: Ebola in DR Congo sees record first-month caseload
stories.summary: The UN says the DR Congo has reported 1,000 Ebola cases, the most ever in the first month of any Ebola outbreak in Africa. Meanwhile, Kenya's health minister says he is halting a US-backed Ebola quarantine center.

stories.title: UN says it will evacuate sailors stranded in Strait of Hormuz, as Rubio warns against tolls

stories.title: Congress passes war powers measure for first time, breaking with Trump over Iran

stories.title: Bondi Beach shooting hero pleads not guilty to alleged assault on his father
stories.summary: Ahmed al Ahmed tackled one of the gunmen who killed 15 people at a Jewish event in December.

stories.title: Clean sweep for Mamdani-backed candidates in New York's Democratic primary

stories.title: Toronto streets fill with Panama and Croatia supporters
stories.summary: Supporters of Panama and Croatia turned downtown Toronto into a sea of flags and chants ahead of their crucial World Cup Group L clash.

stories.title: Dead tree painted red to highlight climate change impact
stories.summary: A painted red oak at Kew Gardens, killed in the 2022 heatwave, stands beside a resilient “Tree of Hope”, highlighting climate change impacts in the UK.

stories.title: Kenya to charge students with murder over deadly school fire
stories.summary: Sixteen pupils, aged between 15 and 18, died when a fire broke out in a dormitory at Utumishi Girls' School last month.

stories.title: UN commission of inquiry says Israel committing genocide in Gaza by deliberately targeting children

stories.title: Von der Leyen to visit Pashinyan in Yerevan to build on Armenia’s pro-EU vote
stories.summary: As the EU rolls out a support package for Armenia, European Commission President von der Leyen is set to meet re-elected Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan in Yerevan after he secured a decisive pro-West mandate from the Armenian people, sources confirmed to Euronews.

stories.title: Who is Abelardo de la Espriella? From controversial lawyer to Colombia's president
stories.summary: The president-elect created his movement just 11 months ago, mirroring the model that worked in Argentina and El Salvador: patriotic talk, a firm hand against crime, and contempt for traditional politics.

stories.title: Europe’s June heatwave – in pictures

stories.title: Europe battles record-breaking heat: is this the new normal? - The Latest
stories.summary: Europe is dealing with a debilitating heatwave, with schools closed, trains cancelled and France holding an emergency meeting after heat-related deaths. António Guterres, the UN chief, is urging the world to act on fossil fuels as the continent braces for record-breaking heat. Lucy Hough speaks to Europe environment correspondent Ajit Niranjan Continue reading...

stories.title: Department of Homeland Security will allow Iran to travel to US early for World Cup match
stories.summary: Iran will be allowed into US 48 hours before crucial game Team Melli have complained about travel conditions The US Department of Homeland Security (DHS) says it will grant Iran extra time to prepare for their World Cup match against Egypt on Friday. The team had planned to lodge an official complaint with Fifa about the “restrictions imposed by the organisers” at the World Cup . Iran have been training in Mexico and were only allowed to enter the United States 24 hours before their first two ma

During a red alert – the highest level – the ministry advises people to ea

stories.title: Rubio insists strait of Hormuz will be toll-free as he arrives for Gulf meeting
stories.summary: US secretary of state seeks to reassure UAE, Kuwait and Bahrain over security and US-Iran ceasefire deal The US secretary of state, Marco Rubio, has said no country, including Iran, would be allowed to charge tolls for shipping in the strait of Hormuz as he sought to reassure US allies in the Gulf that Washington would take a firm line in peace negotiations with Tehran. Rubio is to meet Gulf allies on Tuesday and Wednesday in an attempt to reassure them that the US remains committed to their sec

stories.title: Europe’s heatwave drives electricity prices to new highs as demand soars
stories.summary: Great Britain has paid at least six times the normal price for imported power as millions turn on air conditioning and windfarm output sags The heatwave has prompted a sharp rise in electricity prices across European markets as millions turn to air conditioners and electric fans to battle record high temperatures, which have also caused a string of power plant outages across the continent. Great Britain imported electricity from Europe at more than six times the normal price on Tuesday as the hi

stories.title: Germany news: Merz hails pension reform proposals
stories.summary: Chancellor Merz says a proposed pension reform package should be fully implemented. A survey has shown that almost half of teachers in Germany find pupils' behavior stressful.

stories.title: Europe is spending billions to rearm, so why the delays?
stories.summary: European countries have been trying to scale up their military capabilities, but their current defense production and procurement model is not fit for purpose, say experts.

stories.title: Europe heat wave: France measures hottest-ever day
stories.summary: Monuments in Paris are working on reduced hours, and dozens of people in France have drowned during the heat wave. Britain, France, Italy and Spain have issued red alerts and health warnings. More on DW.

stories.title: Wanted: Jobs, critical minerals as Zambia's election nears
stories.summary: Zambians head to the polls in August with jobs and living costs at the top of their concerns. The outcome could also impact the ongoing global competition for critical minerals and redefine ties with China and the US.

stories.title: Afghan Taliban clamp down on women, smartphones, protests
stories.summary: The Taliban are tightening the screws on women's clothing, shooting at protesters and ordering even their own officials to stop using smartphones. Behind the regime's omnipresent control, there are signs of deep anxiety.

stories.title: Experts fired by Trump resurrect mothballed climate website
stories.summary: Fired US federal workers have revived a defunct climate website — pushing back as the Trump administration escalates cuts to publicly funded science and research.

stories.title: Hong Kong pro-democracy publisher Jimmy Lai honored by DW
stories.summary: Before being imprisoned for 20 years, Jimmy Lai founded Hong Kong's largest pro-democracy newspaper. His son said the award shows that those who fight for the freedom of others "are never alone."

stories.title: Colombia: The 'Tiger's' victory signals a rightward shift
stories.summary: With the election of Abelardo de la Espriella, an ultra-right-wing outsider has won the presidential runoff in Colombia for the first time. The tight victory marks a political shift and could trigger new conflicts.

stories.title: US, Iran give conflicting claims over Hormuz fees
stories.summary: US top diplomat Rubio said Washington will not accept tolls on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. But Iran has said they want to charge "maritime service fees." And Israel and Lebanon are set for talks in the US.

stories.title: Scotland: Sturgeon's ex-husband Peter Murrell jailed for 5 years and 3 months
stories.summary: Peter Murrell pleaded guilty to embezzling funds in his role as chief executive of the Scottish National Party. He held the SNP's purse strings while his then-wife, Nicola Sturgeon, was Scottish First Minister.

stories.title: Lebanon's lose-lose: Is there another way out of conflict?
stories.summary: The new US-Iran peace deal seems to be forcing Lebanon to choose between Israel's sphere of influence or Iran's. It wants neither. But could there be a better alternative, achieved with the help of regional diplomacy?

stories.title: Spider which uses spring trap to capture prey discovered in Australia
stories.summary: Researchers say their hunting method - which allows them to prey on dangerous ants - is unprecedented.

stories.title: Trump anticipates better relationship with Colombia under new leader
stories.summary: Abelardo de la Espriella, who preliminary results suggest is Colombia's next president, had Trump's endorsement.

stories.title: TV pundit apologises after racist comment during Belgium World Cup game

stories.title: Iran says no new commitments on nuclear sites after Vance says inspectors to be invited back
stories.summary: Iran's foreign ministry says it made "no new commitments" on nuclear inspections after talks in Switzerland.

stories.title: 40 mayors worldwide unite to fight back against the AI data centre surge
stories.summary: From Phoenix to Melbourne, a global coalition of city leaders says the AI data centre boom is draining their power, water and land — and they are done waiting.

stories.title: Iran sharply contradicts the US, pushing its own view of framework deal
stories.summary: Shortly after the first high-level US-Iran negotiations ended, discrepancies emerged over what the two sides actually agreed, as Tehran moved to directly contradict US Vice President JD Vance’s statements, prompting US President Donald Trump to reject Iran's claims.

stories.title: Fire at animation studio in India kills at least 15 people
stories.summary: The fire happened in a building housing a student training centre in northern India, raising fresh concerns about fire safety in the country.

stories.title: From cool-down spots to chalk on windows - how Europe is coping with the heat
stories.summary: People across the continent are taking measures to cope with the searing temperatures gripping Europe.

stories.title: Dozens of ships head through Strait of Hormuz after US-Iran deal
stories.summary: Traffic in the waterway has risen since the US and Iran signed a deal aimed at ending the war, including 42 ships on Saturday alone.

stories.title: Armenia and Azerbaijan agree landmark internet deal as peace process continues
stories.summary: Armenia and Azerbaijan’s leading telecom companies agreed to a joint internet transit project as both countries move forward together after decades of war, boosting regional connectivity.

stories.title: From Bolsonaro and Bukele to Fujimori and De la Espriella: Trump and Latin America's rightward turn
stories.summary: Soon after the US president first moved into the White House, his policies and outspoken backing for certain candidates inspired a new wave of Latin American leaders now shaping the continent’s future.

stories.title: US top court says Rastafarian man cannot sue prison guards who cut his dreadlocks
stories.summary: The former Louisiana inmate argued his Rastafarian faith was violated after prison officials focibly shaved off his hair.

stories.title: Drowning deaths soar in France as Europe buckles in peak of heatwave
stories.summary: Forty people have drowned in heatwave-related deaths in France since last Thursday, Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu says.

stories.title: 'We're begging' - Savannah Guthrie pleads for help as details of her mother’s case emerge
stories.summary: Savannah Guthrie spoke on NBC's Today show about a note sent to the media in February regarding her missing mother, Nancy Guthrie, which claimed she had died.

stories.title: Have World Cup changes made final group stage games unfair?

stories.title: Israeli troops kill two in south Lebanon after lull in fighting, authorities say

stories.title: UN launches Hormuz evacuation for 11,000 stranded seafarers

stories.summary: Edgar Lungu's family and the Zambian government remained in dispute 12 months after he died.

stories.summary: On Tuesday, Today show presenter Savannah Guthrie begged anyone with information to come forward, saying, "We are in agony."

stories.title: UNAIDS warns Trump's HIV funding cuts to South Africa could cost lives

stories.title: A 88 éves író, aki erősen kétli, hogy Musk egyáltalán olvas

Bár szerinte kötete olyan témákról szól, mint az emberi életút, a barátság vagy a szavak nélküli kommunikáció, történeteiben visszatérően jelen van a hatalommal való visszaélés és az erőszak következményeinek kérdése.

Amikor több család osztozik egy fürdőszobán, annak állapota közvetlenül hat a mindennapi életminőségre, meghatározza a reggelek és esték szervezettségét, a tisztálkodás lehetőségeit… A Procter & Gamble a Csömöri Családok Átmeneti Otthonában ezt a kulcsfontosságú helyiséget tette komfortossá, hogy az ott élő családoknak ne kelljen

stories.title: A forró nyári estéken is lehet jót aludni – az alvásszakértő tanácsai
stories.summary: Az északi országok fontos ünnepe a Midsummer, a fény ünnepe, az év leghosszabb napján közösen, a szabadban ünneplik a nyár kezdetét. A skandináv gyökerekkel rendelkező JYSK az ünnep apropóján adott fenntartható és inspiráló ötleteket a nyári szabadtéri összejövetelekkel kapcsolatban. Az eseményen a cég alvásszakértőjét, Bárdosi Bernadettet arról is faggattuk, hogy mi a jó alvás titka olyankor, amikor a hálóban melegebb, kint pedig világosabb van, mint szeretnénk.

stories.title: ‘Cruel hoax’: Canadian healthcare staff decry employer’s email promising fake day off
stories.summary: Unions condemn ‘insensitive’ internal cybersecurity test sent to healthcare workers in Newfoundland and Labrador For years, healthcare staff in the Canadian province of Newfoundland and Labrador have felt overworked and underappreciated. Turnover, burnout and thinning resources were pushing workers in the sector to a breaking point. So when the email titled “June Holiday” arrived in thousands of inboxes, they felt a moment of overdue joy. Continue reading...

stories.title: Montreal mayor calls for end to random police checks amid racial profiling investigation

Mayor Soraya Martinez Ferrada told reporters last week that her husband, who is Black, had been repeatedly stopped by police while driving. Continue reading...

stories.title: Why is Bulgaria threatening to veto EU's Russia sanctions?

stories.title: Lost memoir of Hiroshima survivor found after decades in US archive
stories.summary: Written in 1947, Kiyoshi Tanimoto’s account of the horrors of the atomic bomb attack will be published in August and is being made into a film The memoir of a man who survived the horrors of Hiroshima is to be published for the first time this summer after its discovery in a US archive. The 230-page memoir was written almost 80 years ago by Kiyoshi Tanimoto, who witnessed the city’s destruction after the atomic bomb was dropped in 1945. He will now be portrayed in a feature film by Takehiro Hira

stories.summary: Az orosz elnök három korábbi eseményen elhangzott feltételekhez kötné a béketárgyalások megkezdését.

stories.summary: A meccs végén jöttek sorra az angol helyzetek, de Ghána mindent kivédekezett.

stories.title: Trump olyan fegyvert adott Irán kezébe, amit nem tud visszavenni
stories.summary: Az iráni rezsim súlyos veszteségeket szenvedett, de túlélte a háborút, és jelentős engedményeket ért el Trumpnál, miközben a Hormuzi-szoros lezárásával szemrebbenés nélkül fenyegetőzik. Trump láthatóan szabadulni akar a helyzetből, és annak örülhet, hogy a világgazdaság optimistán reagált a múlt heti megállapodásra. Ez azonban nem fedi el a fennmaradó problémákat.

stories.title: Enyhítenek a korlátozáson, már 48 órával az utolsó csoportmeccse előtt az USA-ba mehet az iráni válogatott

stories.title: Germany's Left Party is dreaming big
stories.summary: The socialist Left Party has seen its membership soar and is especially popular with young female voters. Could it soon come to power in the city state of Berlin?

stories.title: The man who knew, but didn't say: Alan Greenspan (1926-2026)
stories.summary: Alan Greenspan’s reputation as head of the US Federal Reserve was legendary — not least because of his reputation for convoluted statements. However, the 2008/9 financial crisis took a toll on his standing.

stories.title: 75 years of Germany's Goethe-Institut
stories.summary: For 75 years, the Goethe-Institut has stood for cultural exchange, education and partnerships around the globe — even under challenging political conditions.

stories.title: Germany and UK rebuilding ties 10 years after Brexit vote
stories.summary: Ten years after the referendum, the two countries are strengthening their ties once again. The stability of these ties is now being put to the test with Keir Starmer to be replaced as prime minister.

stories.title: Meet Andy Burnham, Britain's likely next prime minister
stories.summary: Now that British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced his resignation, all eyes are on Andy Burnham. The former mayor of Manchester is seen as the favorite to succeed Starmer to lead the United Kingdom.

stories.title: World Cup: Amiri another wildcard for Nagelsmann's Germany
stories.summary: The Mainz midfielder with Afghan roots has become a key member of Germany's 2026 World Cup squad. Coach Julian Nagelsmann said Amiri can provide the team with a spark when it's most needed.

stories.title: Germany's pension plans draw praise and outrage
stories.summary: A 33-point plan to reform Germany's pension system has drawn praise and criticism in almost equal measure. Centrist politicians have called it a good compromise, but opposition parties on the left and right are unhappy.

stories.title: More than a unibrow: The enduring appeal of Frida Kahlo
stories.summary: She shunned feminine beauty ideals, was bisexual and a political radical, all while being brutally honest in her art. A major Frida Kahlo exhibition opens at London's Tate Modern.

stories.title: Canal Saint-Martin becomes cooling spot as heatwave grips Paris
stories.summary: Paris faces extreme heat as crowds use fountains and the Canal Saint-Martin to cool off, while authorities impose alerts and safety measures.

stories.title: World's largest kite festival fills Danish skies with 25,000 kites
stories.summary: Thousands gather at Denmark's Fano International Kite Festival as giant dragon, whale and octopus kites fill the skies above the coast.

stories.title: Iran and US make progress on Hormuz despite Trump's explosive threats
stories.summary: JD Vance said 18 hours of talks in Switzerland had "laid a very good foundation" after both sides agreed a mechanism to keep the Strait of Hormuz open and maintain Lebanon's ceasefire, even as Trump threatened to hit Iran "only harder".

stories.title: 'Another day, another leader': Londoners react to Starmer's resignation
stories.summary: People in central London expressed frustration, uncertainty and cautious hope after Keir Starmer announced his resignation as UK prime minister.

stories.title: Japan to increase visa fees by more than five times starting on 1 July
stories.summary: Japan wants to help manage the rising costs of its immigration system by making tourist visas significantly more expensive in the first fee increase in nearly half a century.

stories.title: Messi smashes all-time World Cup goalscoring record with 18 goals for Argentina
stories.summary: Messi has become the highest-scoring player in World Cup history with 18 goals. He now has two more than the previous record holder, former Germany striker Miroslav Klose.

stories.title: Belgium issues visas to Taliban delegation for EU migration talks
stories.summary: The EU is preparing to host Taliban representatives for the first time since the group's return to power in Afghanistan.

stories.title: Honduras to buy Ukrainian drones to fight drug trafficking
stories.summary: The discussions come as the Central American country continues to grapple with drug trafficking and some of the highest levels of violence in the region.

stories.title: Vance says direct talks with Iran set 'good foundation' for deal to end the war
stories.summary: Lower-level officials are continuing to engage in what officials described as “technical talks” in Switzerland following Vance, Ghalibaf and Araghchi’s departure, as the US and Iran look to inch closer to a concrete deal.

stories.title: Kazakhstan brings New Silk Road revival to Brussels as Tokayev visits EU leaders
stories.summary: Kazakhstan's president has arrived in Brussels this week to meet EU leaders and assess the delivery of promises made at last year's EU-Central Asia summit in Samarkand, with the Middle Corridor trade route connecting China to Europe through Central Asia at the top of the agenda.

stories.title: Why was JD Vance in a luxury Swiss resort for talks with Iran?
stories.summary: BBC correspondent Jessica Parker explains some key points of the direct talks between the US vice-president and Iran.

stories.title: Nancy Guthrie ransom note said she died after abduction
stories.summary: Guthrie went missing from her home in Tucson, Arizona, in early February. The FBI is offering a reward up to $100,000 for information leading to her location.

stories.title: Inquiry ordered after building fire kills 15 in north India city
stories.summary: Witnesses described people jumping from windows to escape, while rescue teams broke through a wall to save lives.

stories.title: Brussels residents cool off in fountains as heatwave grips Belgium
stories.summary: People flocked to fountains in Brussels' Parc du Cinquantenaire as temperatures climbed during a heatwave expected to intensify later this week.

stories.title: Police officer and civilian killed in Montreal shooting
stories.summary: Montreal Police named the officer killed in the shooting as 34-year-old Mohamed Lamine Benredouane.

stories.title: Watch: French jets salute US anniversary with Washington flyover
stories.summary: France's elite Patrouille de France aerobatic team performed a ceremonial flyover above some of Washington's most iconic sites as part of celebrations marking 250 years of American independence.

stories.title: Dettol apologises after ad to clean up 'toxic men' backfires in China
stories.summary: The ad sparked accusations of sexism in China, after featuring a man looking for a partner who is "not tainted by other men".

stories.title: Alpine glacier melts faster as second heatwave hits France
stories.summary: The Bossons Glacier in France’s Chamonix Valley is showing fresh signs of rapid retreat as a second heatwave of 2026 grips parts of Europe.

stories.title: Montreal shooting leaves officer, civilian and suspect dead
stories.summary: It was the first time in 24 years that a Montreal police officer was killed in the line of duty, the city's police chief said.

stories.title: Dutch PM apologises for Moluccan soldiers’ mistreatment after Indonesian independence
stories.summary: Rob Jetten acknowledges grief and pain of Moluccan families as crowdfunded monument unveiled in Rotterdam The Dutch prime minister, Rob Jetten, has formally apologised for the “heartless” mistreatment of thousands of Moluccan soldiers who fought for the Dutch colonial army during Indonesia’s struggle for independence. About 12,500 people – men who had served in the Royal Dutch East Indies and their families – came from a group of Indonesian islands to the Netherlands in 1951, many having been gi

stories.title: UK's Starmer mulls 'political realities' after Burnham by-election
stories.summary: A member of UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer's Cabinet said it would be "delusional" to pretend there was no threat to his position. But he also said he had no information suggesting he might quit, as a newspaper reported.

stories.title: Scottish police arrest man over Edinburgh attacks
stories.summary: Five people were injured in the attacks on Friday that were described by Prime Minister Keir Starmer as being motivated by "anti-Muslim hatred."
